learning_salience.rationale: formula_heavy segment defines perplexity as exp(cross-entropy / per-word negative log-likelihood and gives a diagnostic warning that log-base mismatch makes perplexity values incomparable; dominant coverage of supporting KP kp_perplexity_as_a_proxy_metric.
dominant coverage of gateway critical KP kp_vanishing_and_exploding_gradients, with formula_heavy BPTT matrix-power reasoning plus diagnostic signals for NaNs/huge updates and gradient clipping.
dominant coverage of gateway high KP kp_lstm_gates_and_cell_state; formula_heavy walkthrough of forget/input/output gates, cell update, and hidden-state exposure supports procedural assessment.

dominant coverage of critical KP kp_additive_memory_and_gradient_flow_in_lstms; concrete signal is the additive cell-state path and the forget=1/input=0 copy-forward example explaining improved gradient flow.

dominant/substantial coverage of high supporting KPs kp_rnn_lstm_applications_and_conditional_language_models and kp_bidirectional_and_stacked_recurrent_networks, with application signals across tagging, sentence encoding, conditional generation, bidirectional encoders, and stacked RNNs.

kept despite historical opening because the segment contains core_theory signals: Bayes-style statistical MT decomposition and phrase-based MT failure analysis; dominant coverage of supporting medium KP kp_statistical_machine_translation.

dominant coverage of gateway critical KP kp_neural_machine_translation_with_seq2seq_encoder_decoders; concrete signals include encoder-decoder seq2seq formulation, decoder conditioning, autoregressive generation, and end-to-end backpropagation through encoder and decoder.
